decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to maximize the heat spreader's conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since material tuning is saturated, the next best lever is to increase the geometric cross-section of the heat spreader to improve lateral heat distribution. I will increase the spreader width by approximately 14% (from 0.7 to 0.8), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and domain bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing the local thermal resistance and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
Improved heat flux spreading into the base plate
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the left domain boundary (x=0.0); ensure it does not interfere with boundary condition application if the mesh is coarse.
Diminishing returns may occur if the base plate conductivity becomes the new bottleneck after spreader improvement.
Geometric changes require mesh regeneration which could slightly alter numerical precision.
